steps: Before building anything, strip the results spreadsheet down to aggregates only — totals by day, by hour, by challenge grant, new vs. renewing counts — with no donor names, addresses, or giving histories in the file.
Put that stripped file in the folder Claude Code can see, and confirm the original donor-level export stays outside that folder entirely.
Ask Claude to build a Skill — .claude/skills/pledge-recap — that reads the aggregate Sheet and writes the board-ready narrative in your GM's preferred format.
Write the same rule into the SKILL.md itself: this Skill only ever reads aggregate totals, never a donor-level export.
Run the Skill and get the recap — what worked, what fell flat, how it compares to last spring.
Read the recap and confirm every figure is a total or a count, checked against the aggregate Sheet, before it goes to the board.
verify: Open the aggregate file the Skill actually read and search it for an @ sign or anything that looks like a person's name. If either shows up, the file wasn't stripped — redo it before the Skill touches it again.
